description: Cloudflare is a cloud-based platform that provides content delivery network (CDN), Internet security services, and distributed domain name server services. It offers DDoS protection, SSL/TLS, Web Application Firewall (WAF), and performance optimization for websites and APIs.
authentication: API access is secured via API Tokens, Global API Key, or Account ID with Bearer token authentication. OAuth 2.0 is not natively supported for API access.
self_serve: Yes – users can sign up, configure DNS, CDN, and security settings through the Cloudflare dashboard without requiring a sales representative.
api_surface: Cloudflare exposes a comprehensive RESTful API covering DNS, CDN, WAF, firewall rules, load balancing, and account management. The API is documented at https://developers.cloudflare.com/api/
mcp: Cloudflare’s Managed Cloud Platform (MCP) refers to its fully managed infrastructure that abstracts server management, scaling, and security, allowing customers to focus on application logic.
buildability: High – the platform offers a low-code/no-code interface, extensive API, and pre-built integrations, enabling rapid deployment and scaling.
main_blocker: Complexity of configuration and the need to understand multiple layers (DNS, CDN, WAF, firewall rules) can be a barrier for new users.
